data.q: What does 'Accuracy' measure in a classification model?
o: The number of positive predictions made
The ratio of correctly predicted observations to the total observations
The speed at which the model makes a prediction
The difference between the highest and lowest scores
data.e: Accuracy is the most intuitive performance measure, but it can be misleading if the dataset is imbalanced (e.g., 99% of cases are Class A).

data.q: In a medical test, what does 'Recall' (Sensitivity) represent?
o: The ability of the model to find all the positive cases
The ability of the model to avoid false alarms
The total number of patients tested
The time it takes to get results
data.e: Recall answers: 'Of all the people who actually had the disease, how many did we correctly identify?' High recall is critical when the cost of a False Negative is high.

o: The total number of correct predictions
The ratio of correctly predicted positive observations to the total predicted positives
The consistency of the model over time
The depth of the neural network
data.e: Precision answers: 'Of all the people we labeled as having the disease, how many actually had it?' High precision is critical when the cost of a False Positive is high (e.g., spam filters).

o: The maximum accuracy a model can reach
The harmonic mean of Precision and Recall
The square root of the Mean Squared Error
The ratio of training data to testing data
data.e: The F1-Score provides a balance between Precision and Recall. It is particularly useful when you have an uneven class distribution.

data.q: What does the 'ROC Curve' (Receiver Operating Characteristic) visualize?
o: The distribution of features in the dataset
The trade-off between the True Positive Rate and the False Positive Rate at various thresholds
The decrease in loss over training epochs
The relationship between two independent variables
data.e: The ROC curve helps in choosing the best threshold for a classifier. A model with an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 1.0 is perfect.

data.q: Which metric is most suitable for evaluating a Regression model?
o: F1-Score
Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE)
Precision
ROC-AUC
data.e: RMSE measures the average magnitude of the error by taking the square root of the average of squared differences between prediction and actual observation.

o: The ratio of correct classifications
The proportion of variance in the dependent variable that is predictable from the independent variables
The learning rate of the optimizer
The number of layers in a decision tree
data.e: $R^2$ indicates how well the regression model fits the data. A score of 1.0 indicates a perfect fit.

data.q: In a Confusion Matrix, what is a 'True Negative'?
o: An error that the model didn't catch
A case where the model correctly predicted the negative class
A case where the model predicted negative but it was actually positive
A piece of data that was deleted from the set
data.e: A True Negative (TN) is an outcome where the model correctly predicts the negative class (e.g., a healthy person is correctly identified as healthy).

o: Models that are too slow
False predictions that are made with high confidence
Using too many features
Large datasets
data.e: Log Loss measures the performance of a classification model where the prediction input is a probability value between 0 and 1. It heavily penalizes 'confident' wrong answers.

o: A way to encrypt data
A technique where the data is split into K subsets to ensure the model's performance is consistent across different data samples
A method for clustering data into K groups
Training the model on K different computers at once
data.e: Cross-validation provides a more reliable estimate of model performance than a single train/test split by averaging the results of K different trials.

data.q: What is the 'Area Under the Precision-Recall Curve' (PR-AUC) often preferred over ROC-AUC?
o: When the model is a neural network
When dealing with highly imbalanced datasets where the minority class is the focus
When the dataset has more than 1,000 features
When the labels are continuous numbers
data.e: PR-AUC is more informative than ROC-AUC when the positive class is rare, as it doesn't get skewed by a large number of True Negatives.

o: The average of the absolute differences between predictions and actual values
The percentage of total errors
The maximum error in the entire dataset
The error rate of the training set only
data.e: Unlike MSE, MAE does not square the errors, meaning it is more robust to outliers and gives a linear representation of the error magnitude.
